conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your situation.

Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you for the relevant information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm inquiring about. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now, could you please tell me: **Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives at home with me, and we live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. So Alex lives with you in Manchester.

Next, I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One more question: **Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £60,000 per year?**

(This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you have to pay a tax charge on it.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review your situation:

- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000 per year

Based on the official UK government guidance, you meet all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You are responsible for a child under 16, you live with him in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the necessary requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old (he's 8)
- You live with Alex in the UK
- You are responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, you also won't have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, which means you can receive the full benefit without any tax implications.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit if you haven't already done so. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age threshold of 16. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), which satisfies the residency and responsibility requirements. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict with another claim. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and only one person is claiming for the child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. All standard eligibility requirements are met: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Additionally, since neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, they will not be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

Additionally, since neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, they will not be subject to the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
